recipes.name: Classic Omelette

ingredients.item: Filling of choice (tomato, spinach, mushroom, cheese)

steps: Crack eggs into a bowl, add water and salt, whisk until just mixed.
Heat pan on medium heat, add butter or coconut oil — swirl but do not brown.
Add any fillings to sauté, then pour in egg mixture — it will sizzle.
Tip pan so egg covers evenly, cook 30–60 seconds until edges firm but centre remains soft.
Fold one edge over with a spatula and slide onto a plate.
Serve with sourdough toast and fresh fruit.
recipes.nutrition: High protein from eggs · Good fats from butter · Low carb
nutritionSnapshot: Rich in complete protein from free-range eggs
Good fats from butter support brain development
Quick energy with no added sugar
recipes.kidTip: Let kids choose their own filling — it makes them much more likely to eat it!
recipes.ageTip: Cut into strips for toddlers. Older kids can crack the eggs themselves.

recipes.name: Scrambled Eggs on Sourdough
steps: Whisk eggs in a bowl with a pinch of sea salt and 1 tbsp water.
Add butter or coconut oil to pan, heat on medium.
Add egg mixture, stir gently with a spatula until light and fluffy (1–2 mins).
Transfer to a plate, serve on toasted sourdough.
Offer fresh fruit on the side while eggs cook.
recipes.nutrition: Protein-rich · Healthy fats · Slow-release carbs from sourdough
nutritionSnapshot: Complete protein from eggs supports growing muscles
Sourdough provides slower-release energy than regular bread
Butter adds fat-soluble vitamins A and D
recipes.kidTip: Kids can toast the bread and butter it themselves — a great first kitchen job.
recipes.ageTip: Keep eggs very soft and creamy for toddlers. Serve bread without crusts for younger children.

recipes.name: Fried Eggs
steps: Heat pan on medium heat — gentle heat keeps eggs from going rubbery.
Add butter or coconut oil and let it melt.
Crack eggs carefully into the pan.
Cook until whites are set but yolk is still runny (or to preference).
Season with salt and pepper, serve immediately.
recipes.nutrition: Pure protein and healthy fats · No carbs · Quick and filling
nutritionSnapshot: Eggs are one of nature's most complete proteins
Yolk provides choline for brain development
Naturally gluten-free and dairy-free
recipes.kidTip: Let kids pick how they want their yolk — runny or firm. It gives them ownership of the meal.
recipes.ageTip: Fully cooked yolks for toddlers under 2. Runny yolks fine for older children.

recipes.name: Farmers Breakfast
steps: Whisk eggs with a dash of water/milk and salt, set aside.
If sausages uncooked, pan-fry on medium until done, then slice into 1-inch pieces.
In same pan, add potatoes and onion, cook until tender — about 5 minutes.
Return sausage to pan, add egg mixture and stir until almost set.
Sprinkle cheese, cover and cook 1 minute until melted.
Serve hot — great with fresh tomato on the side.
recipes.nutrition: High protein · Slow-release carbs from potato · Filling and balanced
nutritionSnapshot: High protein from eggs and sausage for sustained energy
Potato provides slow-release complex carbohydrates
Cheese adds calcium for growing bones
recipes.kidTip: Let kids sprinkle the cheese — they love that final step!
recipes.ageTip: Cut sausage and potato small for little ones. Great for hungry primary schoolers.

recipes.name: Homemade Granola Bowl
steps: Preheat oven to 150°C / 300°F. Line a baking tray with baking paper.
Mix all dry ingredients in a large bowl.
In a small saucepan, combine oil, honey and vanilla over gentle heat until combined.
Pour wet mix over dry ingredients and stir well to coat.
Spread on baking tray and bake 30–40 minutes, stirring every 15 minutes until golden.
Cool completely then store in an airtight jar for up to 2 weeks.
Serve with Greek yoghurt and fresh berries.
recipes.nutrition: Good fats from nuts and seeds · Slow-release carbs · Great fibre
nutritionSnapshot: Nuts and seeds provide healthy omega fats for brain health
Oats give slow-release energy to power the morning
No artificial additives — sweetened naturally with honey
recipes.kidTip: Kids love making this — let them pour and mix the dry ingredients.
recipes.ageTip: Chop nuts finely for toddlers. Avoid whole nuts under 5 — choking risk.

recipes.name: Greek Yoghurt Protein Bowl
steps: Spoon Greek yoghurt into a bowl.
Top with berries, coconut flakes and seeds.
Drizzle with a little raw honey if desired.
Serve immediately.
recipes.nutrition: High protein from yoghurt · Antioxidants from berries · Gut-friendly probiotics
nutritionSnapshot: Greek yoghurt is packed with protein and gut-healthy probiotics
Berries are rich in antioxidants and vitamin C
Chia seeds add omega-3 fatty acids
recipes.kidTip: Let kids choose and arrange their own toppings — turns breakfast into a creative activity.
recipes.ageTip: Great for all ages. Use whole berries for older children, mashed for babies.

recipes.name: Wild Goat Smoothie
steps: Grind flaxseed in a small blender.
Add yoghurt, blueberries and flaxseed to blender.
Add nut milk or water to desired consistency.
Blend 30 seconds and serve.
recipes.nutrition: Protein from yoghurt · Omega-3 from flaxseed · Antioxidants from berries
nutritionSnapshot: Yoghurt provides protein and probiotics for gut health
Blueberries are rich in brain-boosting antioxidants
Flaxseed adds omega-3 fatty acids
recipes.kidTip: Let kids press the blender button — they feel very involved!
recipes.ageTip: Suitable for all ages. Thin with extra milk for toddlers.

recipes.name: Nutty Start Smoothie
steps: Pour water into blender, add drained soaked almonds.
Blend 30 seconds to make almond milk base.
Add protein powder, banana and vanilla.
Blend a further 30 seconds.
Pour into glass and enjoy — make double and store in a thermos for later.
recipes.nutrition: Plant protein · Good fats from almonds · Natural energy from banana
nutritionSnapshot: Almonds provide healthy fats and vitamin E
Banana gives natural energy and potassium
Dairy-free and gentle on the digestive system
recipes.kidTip: Let kids peel and freeze their own banana pieces the night before.
recipes.ageTip: Check for nut allergies. For toddlers, ensure almonds are very well blended.

recipes.name: Spinach Frittata
steps: Preheat oven to 150°C.
Sauté mushrooms and garlic in oil for 3 minutes until softened.
Whisk eggs with a dash of water, salt and pepper.
Add spinach and spring onion to the pan, pour egg mixture over.
Sprinkle cheese on top.
Bake 30–35 minutes until set and golden.
Slice into portions — great hot or cold.
recipes.nutrition: High protein · Iron from spinach · Good fats from cheese
nutritionSnapshot: Eggs provide complete protein for muscle growth
Spinach is rich in iron and folate for healthy blood
Great for batch cooking — slices keep well in the fridge
recipes.kidTip: Kids can crack the eggs and sprinkle the cheese.
recipes.ageTip: Cut into small squares for little ones. Naturally gluten-free.

recipes.name: Emily's Fish Fingers

ingredients.item: White fish fillets, skinless (cut into finger-sized pieces)

steps: Crack eggs into a bowl, add salt, and mix well.
Crush corn chips in a blender and combine with rice flour in a separate bowl.
Dip each fish piece into the egg, then into the flour/corn chip mix.
Heat pan with coconut oil on medium heat.
Cook fish fingers until golden brown on each side (about 4–5 minutes per side).
Squeeze fresh lemon over and add cracked pepper.
Serve with roasted sweet potato and salad.
recipes.nutrition: High protein from fish · Gluten-free coating · Healthy fats from coconut oil
nutritionSnapshot: White fish is lean and high in complete protein
Rice flour coating is gluten-free and easy to digest
Coconut oil provides medium-chain triglycerides for energy
recipes.kidTip: Kids love crumbing the fish — set up a little assembly line and let them coat each piece.
recipes.ageTip: Make small finger-sized pieces for toddlers. Perfect for all ages.

recipes.name: Garlic Ginger Chicken Thighs
steps: Combine chicken, onion, garlic, ginger, soy sauce, orange juice and pepper in a glass bowl.
Coat chicken well and refrigerate 1 hour or overnight.
Preheat grill to medium-high, oil the grate.
Grill chicken skin-side down 5–6 minutes until marked.
Flip and grill a further 5 minutes until cooked through.
Rest 5 minutes before serving with rice and steamed vegetables.
recipes.nutrition: High protein · Anti-inflammatory ginger · No added sugar
nutritionSnapshot: Chicken thighs are rich in protein and iron
Ginger and garlic have powerful anti-inflammatory properties
Orange juice provides natural vitamin C to support immunity
recipes.kidTip: Let kids help mix the marinade in the bowl.
recipes.ageTip: Remove skin for younger children. Cut into small pieces for toddlers.

recipes.name: Chicken Nuggets (Homemade)

ingredients.item: Chicken breast, cut into nugget chunks

steps: Preheat oven to 205°C / 400°F.
Blend bread with salt, pepper, paprika and parmesan into fine crumbs.
Mix breadcrumbs with rice flour in one bowl.
Whisk eggs with mustard in another bowl.
Dip each chicken chunk in egg mixture, then breadcrumbs.
Place on a baking tray and bake 20 minutes each side.
Serve with natural ketchup or BBQ sauce.
recipes.nutrition: High protein · Baked not fried · No artificial additives
nutritionSnapshot: Baked in the oven — much lower fat than commercially fried nuggets
Chicken breast provides lean complete protein
Parmesan adds calcium and a savoury flavour kids love
recipes.kidTip: Set up an assembly line — kids love crumbing their own nuggets.
recipes.ageTip: Make small bite-sized pieces for toddlers and younger children.

recipes.name: Slow Cooker Lamb Shanks
steps: Season lamb shanks with salt and pepper.
Brown briefly in olive oil in a pan (optional but adds flavour).
Place shanks in slow cooker with all other ingredients.
Cook on low 6–8 hours or high 3–4 hours until falling off the bone.
Serve with mashed sweet potato or spaghetti with spinach sauce.
recipes.nutrition: Very high protein · Rich iron from lamb · Anti-inflammatory herbs
nutritionSnapshot: Lamb is exceptionally rich in iron, zinc and B12
Slow cooking makes protein very easy to digest
Perfect for children with small appetites — the meat is incredibly tender
recipes.kidTip: Let kids tear the soft meat off the bone at the table — they love it!
recipes.ageTip: Meat falls off the bone — perfect texture for all ages including toddlers.

recipes.name: Beef Burgers with Sourdough
steps: Mix beef mince with egg, garlic, salt and pepper. Form into 4 patties.
Cook patties in a hot pan 3–4 minutes each side until cooked through.
Meanwhile, sauté onions in butter until golden.
Toast sourdough and butter lightly.
Layer patty on toast with sautéed onions, grated carrot, tomato and beetroot.
Serve open or as a sandwich.
recipes.nutrition: High protein from beef · Slow-release carbs from sourdough · Fibre from veggies
nutritionSnapshot: Beef provides iron, zinc and B12 essential for children
Sourdough is easier to digest than regular bread
Beetroot and carrot add natural sweetness kids enjoy
recipes.kidTip: Let kids build their own burger — choosing their own toppings makes them adventurous eaters.
recipes.ageTip: Cut burger into small pieces for toddlers. Remove tough beetroot skin.

recipes.name: Simple Salmon Steaks
steps: Pat salmon dry with paper towel and season with salt and pepper.
Heat oil in pan over medium-high heat.
Cook salmon 3–4 minutes each side until cooked through.
Add garlic and lemon in last minute of cooking.
Serve with sweet potato mash and roasted vegetables.
recipes.nutrition: Very high protein · Rich omega-3 · Anti-inflammatory
nutritionSnapshot: Salmon is one of the best sources of omega-3 for brain development
High quality protein supports growth in children
Naturally gluten-free and dairy-free
recipes.kidTip: Let kids squeeze the lemon over the salmon at the table.
recipes.ageTip: Remove all bones carefully before serving. Flake into pieces for younger children.

recipes.name: Minty Lamb Koftas with Pitta
steps: Mix lamb mince with mint, garlic, cumin, salt and pepper.
Shape into small oval koftas or sausage shapes around skewers.
Grill or pan-fry on medium-high heat 8–10 minutes, turning occasionally.
Warm pitta bread lightly.
Serve koftas in pitta with salad or zucchini fritters.
recipes.nutrition: High protein from lamb · Slow-release carbs from pitta · Iron-rich
nutritionSnapshot: Lamb is rich in iron and zinc for immune function
Mint adds natural flavour without sugar
Pitta provides sustained energy for active kids
recipes.kidTip: Kids love rolling the meat into kofta shapes — great for fine motor skills.
recipes.ageTip: Remove from skewers and cut small for toddlers. Serve pitta as strips for dipping.

recipes.name: Lentil Pasta Sauce

ingredients.item: Tinned brown lentils, drained and rinsed

steps: Heat olive oil in a large pan, add onion and garlic, cook 2 minutes.
Add carrot, mushrooms, zucchini and capsicum, cook 5 minutes.
Add oregano, tomatoes and lentils.
Simmer 15 minutes. Season to taste.
Meanwhile cook pasta per packet instructions.
Serve sauce over pasta with parmesan if desired.
recipes.nutrition: Plant protein from lentils · High fibre · Full of vegetables
nutritionSnapshot: Lentils provide plant protein and iron for vegetarian families
Five vegetables hidden in one sauce — kids won't notice
High fibre supports healthy digestion
recipes.kidTip: Let kids help chop soft vegetables with a child-safe knife.
recipes.ageTip: Blend sauce for toddlers who reject chunks. Older kids enjoy the texture.

recipes.name: Beef Stir-Fry with Rice
steps: Cook rice using rice cooker or pot, set aside.
Heat coconut oil in wok on high heat.
Add beef strips, cook until browned — about 3 minutes.
Add garlic and ginger, stir 30 seconds.
Add vegetables, stir-fry 3–4 minutes until tender-crisp.
Add tamari/soy sauce and toss to coat.
Serve over rice.
recipes.nutrition: High protein from beef · Balanced carbs from rice · Vegetables for fibre
nutritionSnapshot: Beef provides iron and zinc essential for growing children
Rice gives sustained energy throughout the evening
Ginger and garlic support immune function
recipes.kidTip: Let kids choose which vegetables go in — they're more likely to eat what they picked.
recipes.ageTip: Cut beef into very small pieces for toddlers. Reduce soy sauce for younger children.

recipes.name: Roasted Potato or Sweet Potato Wedges
steps: Pre-boil potatoes, cool in fridge (or use cold leftover potatoes).
Preheat oven to 205°C / 400°F.
Cut potatoes into small cubes or wedges.
Toss with olive oil, parmesan, salt, garlic powder, paprika and pepper.
Spread in a single layer on baking tray.
Roast 20 minutes until crispy outside, soft inside.
Cook extra and freeze for school lunchboxes.
recipes.nutrition: Slow-release carbs · Vitamin C from potato · Good fats from oil
nutritionSnapshot: Sweet potato is rich in beta-carotene and vitamin A
Roasting is much healthier than deep frying
Batch cook and freeze for easy lunchbox additions
recipes.kidTip: Kids can toss the potatoes in the bowl with all the seasonings.
recipes.ageTip: Make bite-sized cubes for toddlers. Sweet potato is naturally sweeter and usually well accepted.

recipes.name: Salmon with Sweet Potato Mash
steps: Boil sweet potato cubes until tender (15–20 min). Drain.
Mash with butter, cream, salt and honey until smooth.
Season salmon with salt and pepper.
Heat coconut oil in pan and cook salmon 3–4 min each side.
Serve salmon on a bed of greens alongside sweet potato mash.
recipes.nutrition: Omega-3 from salmon · Beta-carotene from sweet potato · Iron from greens
nutritionSnapshot: Salmon is the richest food source of omega-3 for brain development
Sweet potato mash is naturally sweet — kids love it
Greens add iron, calcium and vitamin K
recipes.kidTip: Let kids mash the sweet potato — it's a great sensory activity.
recipes.ageTip: Remove all bones carefully. Sweet potato mash is perfect texture for all ages.

recipes.name: Simple Butter Brown Rice
steps: Combine rice, water, butter and salt in rice cooker or pot.
If using rice cooker: set to Cook.
If using stovetop: bring to boil, reduce to lowest heat, cover and cook 30 minutes.
Do not lift lid during cooking.
Rest 5–10 minutes after cooking, then fluff with a fork.
Also great for cooking quinoa (same water ratio) and oats.
recipes.nutrition: Slow-release energy · Simple and digestible · Versatile base
nutritionSnapshot: Rice provides gentle slow-release carbohydrates
Butter adds fat-soluble vitamins
Plain rice is well tolerated by even the fussiest eaters
recipes.kidTip: A rice cooker is one of the best investments for family cooking — kids can help measure the water.
recipes.ageTip: Suitable for all ages. White rice is gentler on small tummies.

recipes.name: Turkey Chili
steps: Heat oil in large pot, sauté onion and garlic until soft.
Add turkey mince and cook until browned.
Add capsicum and cook 2 minutes.
Add tomatoes, beans, cumin and paprika.
Simmer 25–30 minutes until thickened.
Season and serve over rice or with wholegrain bread.
recipes.nutrition: High lean protein from turkey · Fibre from beans · Lycopene from tomatoes
nutritionSnapshot: Turkey is one of the leanest protein sources available
Black beans add fibre and plant protein
Lycopene in tomatoes supports heart health
recipes.kidTip: Let kids help stir and season — they can smell the spices before adding.
recipes.ageTip: Go easy on chilli for toddlers. Mash beans slightly for younger children.

recipes.name: Grilled Chicken Breast with Potato
steps: Boil potatoes until tender, slice and pan-fry in butter until golden.
Season chicken with salt, pepper and garlic powder.
Grill chicken 5–6 minutes each side until cooked through.
Sauté spinach in coconut oil for 2 minutes until wilted.
Serve chicken with potato slices and sautéed spinach.
recipes.nutrition: High lean protein · Iron from spinach · Balanced meal
nutritionSnapshot: Chicken breast is one of the leanest protein sources
Spinach provides iron and folate for healthy blood
Potato gives slow-release energy for growing children
recipes.kidTip: Let kids help slice the cooked potato with a table knife.
recipes.ageTip: Cut chicken into small pieces for toddlers. Spinach wilted is easy for all ages.

recipes.name: Baked Wild Salmon with Roasted Potatoes
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C.
Toss potato cubes in olive oil, salt and garlic. Roast 25 minutes.
Place salmon on a lined tray, drizzle with olive oil, lemon juice and herbs.
Add salmon to oven for final 15 minutes of potato cooking time.
Serve salmon with roasted potatoes and a green salad.
recipes.nutrition: Omega-3 rich salmon · Slow-release carbs · Heart-healthy
nutritionSnapshot: Wild salmon has higher omega-3 content than farmed salmon
Roasted potatoes retain more nutrients than mashed or boiled
Lemon adds vitamin C which helps absorb iron from the fish
recipes.kidTip: Kids can tear fresh dill or herbs to scatter over the salmon.
recipes.ageTip: Check carefully for bones. Flake salmon onto plates for younger children.

recipes.name: Sardine Avocado Salad
steps: Arrange salad leaves, cucumber and tomatoes in a bowl.
Slice avocado and add to salad.
Drain sardines and place on top.
Drizzle with olive oil and lemon juice.
Season with salt and pepper and serve.
recipes.nutrition: Omega-3 from sardines · Heart-healthy fats from avocado · Iron-rich
nutritionSnapshot: Sardines are one of the most concentrated sources of omega-3
Avocado provides monounsaturated fats for brain health
Quick and requires no cooking — ready in 10 minutes
recipes.kidTip: Introduce sardines slowly — start with mild brands in olive oil.
recipes.ageTip: Best for older children who enjoy salad textures. Mash sardine and avocado together for younger ones.

recipes.name: Volcano Brownies (Beetroot)
steps: Preheat oven to 180°C/350°F.
Cream butter and sweetener until light and pale.
Add eggs one at a time, mixing between each.
Fold in cocoa powder, baking powder, almond flour, vanilla and grated beetroot.
Pour into a buttered 20x20cm lined baking dish.
Bake 20–25 minutes until cooked in the centre.
Cool before cutting into squares.
recipes.nutrition: Gluten-free · Natural sweetener · Hidden beetroot for nutrients
nutritionSnapshot: Almond flour provides healthy fats and is gluten-free
Beetroot adds natural sweetness and is rich in folate
Sweetened with erythritol or coconut sugar instead of refined sugar
recipes.kidTip: Kids love grating the beetroot — their hands turn pink which is hilarious!
recipes.ageTip: Cut small for little ones. A great way to introduce beetroot.

recipes.name: Coconut Macaroons
steps: Clean bowl thoroughly — any oil will prevent egg whites from whisking.
Whisk egg whites with sweetener to form stiff peaks.
Fold in vanilla and coconut gently.
Roll spoonfuls into small balls and place on lined baking tray.
Bake at 180°C for 10–12 minutes until golden brown.
Cool before serving.
recipes.nutrition: Gluten-free · Dairy-free · Natural sweetener · Good fats from coconut
nutritionSnapshot: Coconut provides medium-chain triglycerides for quick energy
Sweetened without refined sugar using erythritol
Only 4 ingredients — simple and clean
recipes.kidTip: Kids love rolling the mixture into balls — a great hands-on treat to make together.
recipes.ageTip: Suitable for all ages. Check for egg allergies.

recipes.name: Bounty Bars (Homemade)
steps: For base: heat coconut milk, sweetener and coconut oil in saucepan until combined.
Mix in desiccated coconut and pour onto lined tray 1–2cm thick.
Set in fridge until firm, then cut into bars.
For coating: heat coconut oil, cacao and sweetener until combined. Cool slightly.
Coat bars in chocolate, place on tray.
Refrigerate until set, add a second coat of chocolate.
Store in fridge.
recipes.nutrition: No refined sugar · Healthy fats from coconut · Dairy-free
nutritionSnapshot: Sweetened naturally with erythritol — no sugar spike
Coconut oil provides healthy medium-chain fats
Dairy-free and gluten-free — suitable for most dietary needs
recipes.kidTip: Kids love the dipping step — set up a chocolate dipping station!
recipes.ageTip: Cut into small pieces for younger children. Best kept cold.

recipes.name: Ginger Nut Biscuits
steps: Cream butter and sweetener until smooth and pale.
Add egg and beat until smooth.
Add fresh ginger, ground ginger, salt and almond flour. Mix well.
Drop spoonfuls onto lined baking tray.
Bake at 180°C for 12–15 minutes until golden.
Cool completely before serving.
recipes.nutrition: Gluten-free · Anti-inflammatory ginger · Natural sweetener
nutritionSnapshot: Almond flour provides healthy fats and is naturally gluten-free
Fresh ginger is a powerful anti-inflammatory and digestive aid
No refined sugar — sweetened with erythritol
recipes.kidTip: Kids can roll dough into balls and flatten onto the tray.
recipes.ageTip: Soft texture suitable for all ages. Fresh ginger mild in small amounts.

recipes.name: Avocado & Egg Toast
steps: Toast bread until golden.
Mash avocado with lemon juice, salt and pepper.
Fry or poach eggs to preference.
Spread avocado on toast, top with egg.
Season with chilli flakes and extra salt if desired.
recipes.nutrition: Healthy fats from avocado · Protein from eggs · Slow-release carbs
nutritionSnapshot: Avocado provides heart-healthy monounsaturated fats
Eggs deliver complete protein for sustained energy
Sourdough is easier to digest than regular bread
recipes.kidTip: Let kids mash the avocado — it's a satisfying job for little hands.
recipes.ageTip: Skip chilli for toddlers. Cut toast into soldiers for younger children.

recipes.name: Overnight Oats with Berries
steps: Combine oats, yoghurt, milk, chia seeds, honey and cinnamon in a jar.
Stir well, cover and refrigerate overnight.
In the morning, top with fresh berries.
Add extra milk if needed to reach desired consistency.
Serve cold — ready in seconds on busy mornings.
recipes.nutrition: Slow-release oats · Protein from yoghurt · Omega-3 from chia
nutritionSnapshot: Oats provide beta-glucan fibre for healthy cholesterol
Chia seeds swell overnight adding omega-3 and extra fibre
Make the night before — zero morning effort
recipes.kidTip: Let kids build their own jar the night before — they look forward to it in the morning.
recipes.ageTip: Suitable for all ages. Mash berries for babies and toddlers.

recipes.name: High Protein Pancakes
steps: Blend oats into a flour in a blender.
Add eggs, banana, yoghurt, baking powder and cinnamon. Blend until smooth.
Heat coconut oil in pan on medium heat.
Pour small rounds of batter and cook 2 minutes each side.
Serve with fresh berries and a drizzle of raw honey.
recipes.nutrition: High protein · Gluten-free · Natural sweetness from banana
nutritionSnapshot: Oat flour is gluten-free and high in slow-release carbohydrates
Eggs and yoghurt together provide a full amino acid profile
No added sugar — naturally sweetened by banana
recipes.kidTip: Kids love flipping the pancakes — a great first cooking skill.
recipes.ageTip: Make silver-dollar size for toddlers. Easy to pick up and eat.

recipes.name: Cottage Cheese Berry Bowl
steps: Spoon cottage cheese into a bowl.
Top with berries and oats.
Sprinkle with flaxseeds.
Drizzle honey to finish.
Serve immediately.
recipes.nutrition: Very high protein · High fibre · Calcium-rich
nutritionSnapshot: Cottage cheese is one of the highest protein dairy foods
Oats add slow-release carbohydrates and beta-glucan fibre
Flaxseeds provide plant-based omega-3 fatty acids
recipes.kidTip: Let kids choose which berries go on top.
recipes.ageTip: Excellent for all ages. The texture is soft and easy to eat.

recipes.name: Banana Oat Porridge
steps: Combine oats and milk in a pot over medium heat.
Stir regularly for 5 minutes until oats are creamy.
Stir in cinnamon and honey.
Pour into bowls and top with banana, nuts and chia seeds.
Serve warm — add extra milk if too thick.
recipes.nutrition: Slow-release energy · Heart-healthy oats · Natural sweetness
nutritionSnapshot: Oats are one of the best breakfast foods for sustained energy
Banana adds potassium and natural sweetness without refined sugar
Walnuts provide omega-3 fatty acids for brain health
recipes.kidTip: Let kids peel and slice the banana with a table knife.
recipes.ageTip: Thin with extra milk for babies. Perfect warming breakfast for all ages.

recipes.name: Boiled Eggs with Soldiers
steps: Place eggs in cold water in a pot. Bring to full boil.
Turn off heat, cover and set timer: 4 min runny, 6 min medium, 8 min hard.
Meanwhile toast and butter bread, cut into soldiers.
Place eggs in egg cups, slice top off.
Serve with toast soldiers for dipping.
recipes.nutrition: Complete protein · Healthy fats in yolk · Slow-release carbs
nutritionSnapshot: Egg yolk contains choline essential for brain development
Runny yolk retains more nutrients than fully cooked
Sourdough soldiers provide slow-release energy
recipes.kidTip: Kids love dipping soldiers into the runny yolk — a classic for a reason!
recipes.ageTip: Fully cooked yolks for children under 2. Runny yolk fine for older children.

recipes.name: Smoked Salmon & Cream Cheese Bagel
steps: Slice and toast bagels.
Spread cream cheese generously on each half.
Layer smoked salmon and cucumber slices.
Squeeze lemon juice and add black pepper.
Serve immediately.
recipes.nutrition: Omega-3 from salmon · Protein from salmon and cream cheese · Calcium
nutritionSnapshot: Smoked salmon is rich in omega-3 DHA for brain and eye development
Cream cheese provides calcium and healthy fats
Wholegrain bagel gives sustained energy
recipes.kidTip: Let kids help spread the cream cheese.
recipes.ageTip: Best for primary-aged children. Cut into quarters for easier eating.

recipes.name: Quinoa Porridge
steps: Rinse quinoa well under cold water.
Combine quinoa and milk in a pot, bring to gentle boil.
Reduce heat, add cinnamon, cover and cook 15 minutes until creamy.
Stir in honey.
Serve topped with fruit and pumpkin seeds.
recipes.nutrition: Complete protein grain · Gluten-free · More protein than oats
nutritionSnapshot: Quinoa is a complete protein — unusual for a grain
Naturally gluten-free and easy to digest
Higher in protein and nutrients than regular porridge oats
recipes.kidTip: Let kids choose their own toppings from a small selection of options.
recipes.ageTip: Suitable for all ages including babies starting solids.

recipes.name: Green Lantern Smoothie
steps: Place all ingredients into a blender.
Blend for 30 seconds until smooth.
Add more water if too thick.
Pour into a glass and serve immediately.
recipes.nutrition: Plant protein · Healthy fats from avocado · Liver-supporting greens
nutritionSnapshot: Cucumber and greens support liver detoxification
Avocado provides healthy fats and vitamin E
Great way to get vegetables into children who refuse to eat them
recipes.kidTip: Tell kids it's a superhero smoothie — the green colour is the power!
recipes.ageTip: Best for preschool age and up. Ensure protein powder is child-appropriate.

recipes.name: Mackerel Breakfast Plate
steps: Roast or steam sweet potato until tender.
Pan-fry or grill mackerel fillet 3–4 minutes each side.
Steam or roast mixed vegetables.
Plate mackerel alongside cottage cheese, sweet potato and vegetables.
Season and drizzle with olive oil.
recipes.nutrition: Omega-3 rich · Very high protein · Beta-carotene from sweet potato
nutritionSnapshot: Mackerel is exceptionally high in omega-3 DHA for brain development
Combined with cottage cheese gives one of the highest protein breakfast options
Sweet potato provides beta-carotene converted to vitamin A
recipes.kidTip: Introduce mackerel gradually — start with mild tinned versions in olive oil.
recipes.ageTip: Best for school-aged children. Remove all bones carefully.

recipes.name: Egg & Vegetable Muffins
steps: Preheat oven to 180°C. Grease a 12-hole muffin tin.
Whisk eggs with salt and pepper.
Divide vegetables evenly between muffin holes.
Pour egg mixture over vegetables.
Top with cheese if using.
Bake 20 minutes until set and golden.
Cool and store in fridge up to 4 days — great lunchbox food.
recipes.nutrition: High protein · Packed with vegetables · Gluten-free
nutritionSnapshot: Each muffin delivers protein from egg to fuel growing bodies
Three vegetables baked in — easy way to increase intake
Perfect batch cook — make 12 and use all week
recipes.kidTip: Let kids spoon the vegetables into the muffin tin holes.
recipes.ageTip: Perfect for all ages. Soft texture ideal for toddlers. Great for lunchboxes.

recipes.name: Roast Chicken with Vegetables
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C.
Rub chicken with olive oil, salt, pepper and herbs.
Scatter vegetables and garlic around chicken in roasting tin.
Drizzle vegetables with oil and season.
Roast 1h 20min until juices run clear (75°C internal temp).
Rest 10 minutes before carving.
Use leftover chicken for next day's lunch.
recipes.nutrition: High protein · Rich in B vitamins · Balanced with carbs and veg
nutritionSnapshot: Chicken is a lean complete protein rich in B vitamins
Roasting vegetables retains more nutrients than boiling
Leftover chicken makes excellent next-day lunchboxes
recipes.kidTip: Give kids a job drumming on the table while they wait — build anticipation! Let older kids carve with help.
recipes.ageTip: Pull meat off bone for toddlers. Breast meat is softer for younger children.

recipes.name: Beef Mince & Sweet Potato Bowl
steps: Roast sweet potato cubes at 200°C for 30 minutes.
Sauté onion and garlic in olive oil until soft.
Add beef mince and cook until browned.
Season with cumin, paprika and salt.
Add spinach and stir until wilted.
Serve mince over roasted sweet potato.
recipes.nutrition: High protein from beef · Beta-carotene from sweet potato · Iron from spinach
nutritionSnapshot: Beef mince provides iron and zinc for immune function
Sweet potato is rich in beta-carotene and vitamin A
Spinach adds folate and iron — wilting reduces volume so kids eat more
recipes.kidTip: Let kids scoop the mince over their own sweet potato bowl.
recipes.ageTip: Naturally soft textures — great for all ages. Mash sweet potato for toddlers.

recipes.name: Salmon & Roasted Carrots
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C.
Toss carrots with coconut oil, honey, garlic and salt on a baking tray.
Roast carrots 15 minutes.
Add salmon fillets to tray, season with salt, pepper and lemon.
Roast a further 15 minutes until salmon is cooked through.
Serve with rice or quinoa.
recipes.nutrition: Omega-3 rich · Beta-carotene from carrots · Anti-inflammatory
nutritionSnapshot: Salmon is the richest food source of omega-3 DHA
Honey-roasted carrots are naturally sweet — kids love them
Baking salmon is the gentlest cooking method preserving most nutrients
recipes.kidTip: Let kids drizzle the honey over the carrots.
recipes.ageTip: Flake salmon for younger children. Carrots become very soft when roasted.

recipes.name: Lamb Meatballs with Roasted Veg
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C.
Mix lamb mince with egg, garlic, cumin, salt and pepper.
Roll into 20 meatballs, place on lined baking tray.
Toss vegetables in coconut oil on a separate tray.
Roast both trays 25–30 minutes, turning meatballs halfway.
Serve meatballs with roasted vegetables and quinoa or rice.
recipes.nutrition: Iron-rich lamb · Fibre from vegetables · Balanced meal
nutritionSnapshot: Lamb is exceptionally high in iron and zinc
Meatballs are an excellent way to introduce lamb to children
Roasted vegetables develop natural sweetness kids enjoy
recipes.kidTip: Rolling meatballs is a perfect job for little hands.
recipes.ageTip: Cut meatballs in half for toddlers. Soft texture easy for all ages.

recipes.name: Chicken & Vegetable Soup
steps: Sauté onion and garlic in olive oil until soft.
Add chicken and cook until sealed on outside.
Add all vegetables, stock, salt and pepper.
Bring to boil, reduce heat and simmer 30 minutes.
Remove chicken, shred with forks, return to pot.
Serve with crusty bread and fresh parsley.
recipes.nutrition: High protein · Immune-boosting · Gut-healing bone broth base
nutritionSnapshot: Chicken soup has been shown to have genuine anti-inflammatory properties
Bone broth base supports gut health and immunity
One of the most comforting and nourishing meals for sick children
recipes.kidTip: Let kids add the herbs to the pot and stir.
recipes.ageTip: Blend smooth for toddlers or serve with small soft chunks. Perfect for all ages.

recipes.name: Pork Chops with Apple & Sweet Potato
steps: Season pork chops with salt, pepper and thyme.
Heat coconut oil in pan over medium-high heat.
Cook chops 4–5 minutes each side until cooked through.
In same pan, cook apple slices 2 minutes until softened.
Serve chops with apple slices and sweet potato on the side.
recipes.nutrition: High protein from pork · Natural sweetness from apple · Beta-carotene
nutritionSnapshot: Pork is a rich source of B vitamins especially B1 for energy metabolism
Apple adds natural sweetness and fibre — a classic pairing
Sweet potato provides slow-release energy and vitamin A
recipes.kidTip: Kids love the sweet apple paired with the savoury pork.
recipes.ageTip: Cut chop meat off the bone for younger children. Apple becomes very soft when cooked.

recipes.name: Salmon Quinoa Bowl
steps: Cook quinoa per packet instructions.
Pan-fry salmon in coconut oil 3–4 minutes each side.
Wilt spinach in same pan for 1 minute.
Slice avocado.
Build bowls: quinoa base, spinach, salmon and avocado.
Drizzle with lemon juice and season.
recipes.nutrition: Complete protein quinoa + salmon · Omega-3 · Healthy fats
nutritionSnapshot: Salmon and quinoa together deliver all essential amino acids
Avocado provides healthy monounsaturated fats
Spinach adds iron which is better absorbed alongside lemon vitamin C
recipes.kidTip: Let kids build their own bowl — great for encouraging adventurous eating.
recipes.ageTip: Flake salmon for younger children. Remove avocado skin carefully.

recipes.name: Chicken Thigh Potato Wedges & Salad
steps: Preheat oven to 210°C.
Toss wedges in half the oil, garlic powder, paprika, salt.
Rub chicken with remaining oil and seasoning.
Place chicken and wedges on separate trays.
Roast 40 minutes until chicken skin is crispy and wedges golden.
Serve with simple green salad.
recipes.nutrition: High protein · Iron-rich chicken · Slow-release potato
nutritionSnapshot: Chicken thighs are richer in iron and zinc than breast meat
Baked wedges are much healthier than deep-fried chips
A complete family meal from just a handful of ingredients
recipes.kidTip: Kids love dipping wedges — serve with a small pot of natural ketchup.
recipes.ageTip: Remove chicken from bone for younger children. Wedges soft inside.

recipes.name: Beef & Spinach Stir Fry
steps: Heat wok until very hot, add coconut oil.
Add beef strips in batches, cook 2 minutes until browned. Remove.
Add garlic and ginger, stir 30 seconds.
Add spinach and tamari, stir until wilted 2 minutes.
Return beef to wok, toss together.
Serve over brown rice, scatter sesame seeds.
recipes.nutrition: High iron from beef and spinach · Complete protein · Anti-inflammatory ginger
nutritionSnapshot: Beef and spinach together provide exceptional iron intake
Vitamin C in spinach helps absorb iron from the beef
Ginger and garlic are natural anti-inflammatory and immune boosters
recipes.kidTip: Let kids shake sesame seeds over their bowl.
recipes.ageTip: Cut beef very small for toddlers. Reduce tamari for younger children.

recipes.name: High Protein Lasagne
steps: Preheat oven to 180°C.
Sauté onion and garlic in oil, add mince and cook until browned.
Add tomatoes, spinach and oregano. Simmer 15 minutes.
Layer in baking dish: meat sauce, lasagne sheets, cottage cheese.
Repeat layers ending with cottage cheese and parmesan on top.
Bake covered 35 minutes, uncovered 10 minutes until golden.
Rest 10 minutes before slicing.
recipes.nutrition: Very high protein · Calcium from cottage cheese · Hidden spinach
nutritionSnapshot: Cottage cheese replaces white sauce — far higher in protein
Spinach hidden in the meat layer adds iron and folate
Great batch cook — feeds 6 and reheats perfectly
recipes.kidTip: Kids can layer the lasagne sheets — they love the building element.
recipes.ageTip: Soft texture suitable for all ages. Cut into small squares for toddlers.

recipes.name: Tuna & Potato Fish Cakes
steps: Drain tuna well and flake into a bowl.
Add cold mashed potato, egg, spring onions, parsley and lemon zest.
Season and mix well. Shape into 8 patties.
Refrigerate 15 minutes to firm up.
Cook in coconut oil 3–4 minutes each side until golden.
Serve with green salad and lemon wedges.
recipes.nutrition: High protein from tuna · Slow-release carbs · Omega-3
nutritionSnapshot: Tinned tuna is a budget-friendly and convenient protein source
Potato binding makes these naturally gluten-free
Easy to make ahead and reheat — great for busy families
recipes.kidTip: Kids love shaping the patties — flour their hands slightly to prevent sticking.
recipes.ageTip: Soft texture perfect for all ages. Check carefully for any bones.

recipes.name: Chicken & Avocado Salad
steps: Arrange salad leaves in a large bowl.
Add cucumber, tomatoes and sliced chicken.
Slice avocado and add on top.
Drizzle with olive oil and lemon juice.
Season and toss gently to combine.
recipes.nutrition: High protein · Healthy fats · Nutrient-dense greens
nutritionSnapshot: Using leftover chicken makes this a zero-waste meal
Avocado provides heart-healthy fats and vitamin E
A balanced meal with protein, fat and plenty of vegetables
recipes.kidTip: Use leftover roast chicken — perfect way to stretch one meal into two.
recipes.ageTip: Best for older children who enjoy salad. Chop ingredients very small for younger ones.

recipes.name: Slow Cooker Beef Stew
steps: Brown beef briefly in olive oil (optional but adds depth of flavour).
Place all ingredients in slow cooker.
Cook on low 7–8 hours or high 3–4 hours.
Beef should be falling-apart tender.
Adjust seasoning before serving.
Serve with crusty bread or over rice.
recipes.nutrition: High iron from beef · Vitamin-rich vegetables · Warming and comforting
nutritionSnapshot: Slow cooking breaks down collagen creating gut-healing gelatin
Beef provides iron, zinc and B12 in their most bioavailable forms
Add everything in the morning — dinner is ready when you get home
recipes.kidTip: Kids can help load the slow cooker in the morning — so exciting to come home to dinner ready!
recipes.ageTip: Meat is very tender — perfect for all ages. Mash potatoes and carrots for toddlers.

recipes.name: Roast Leg of Lamb with Vegetables
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C.
Make small cuts in lamb, push in garlic slivers and rosemary.
Rub lamb with olive oil, lemon, salt and pepper.
Scatter potatoes and zucchini around lamb in roasting tin.
Roast 20 mins, reduce to 180°C, continue 1h 20min for medium.
Rest lamb 15 minutes before carving.
Serve with pan juices poured over.
recipes.nutrition: Very high protein · Iron and zinc from lamb · B12 rich
nutritionSnapshot: Lamb is one of the richest sources of iron for growing children
The pan juices contain valuable fat-soluble vitamins
Perfect Sunday meal — leftovers last the whole week
recipes.kidTip: Let kids help push the rosemary into the holes in the lamb.
recipes.ageTip: Cut meat very small for toddlers. Lamb is tender and easy to eat.

recipes.name: Chicken & Cashew Stir Fry
steps: Heat wok with coconut oil on high heat.
Cook chicken in batches until golden, set aside.
Add garlic, capsicum and broccoli, stir-fry 3 minutes.
Return chicken to wok with cashews.
Add tamari and sesame oil, toss to coat.
Serve immediately over rice.
recipes.nutrition: High protein · Healthy fats from cashews · Vitamin C from capsicum
nutritionSnapshot: Cashews add healthy fats and a satisfying crunch
Broccoli provides vitamin C which doubles iron absorption
Ready in under 20 minutes — perfect weeknight dinner
recipes.kidTip: Kids love the crunch of the cashews. Let them count them out.
recipes.ageTip: Chop cashews for under 5s — whole nuts are a choking risk. Remove broccoli stems.

recipes.name: Zucchini & Chickpea Fritters
steps: Grate zucchini, squeeze out excess water in a clean towel.
Mash chickpeas roughly, leaving some texture.
Combine zucchini, chickpeas, eggs, feta and garlic. Season well.
Shape into patties.
Cook in olive oil 3–4 minutes each side until golden.
Serve with Greek yoghurt dipping sauce.
recipes.nutrition: Plant protein from chickpeas · Calcium from feta · Fibre-rich
nutritionSnapshot: Chickpeas provide plant protein and slow-release carbohydrates
Zucchini adds fibre and vitamins with a mild flavour kids enjoy
Naturally gluten-free using chickpeas as the binder
recipes.kidTip: Kids love helping squeeze water out of the zucchini — it's surprisingly satisfying!
recipes.ageTip: Soft texture perfect for all ages. Great lunchbox food cold.

recipes.name: Quinoa & Chicken Bowl
steps: Season and cook chicken in olive oil 5–6 minutes each side. Slice.
Wilt spinach in same pan with garlic powder.
Cook capsicum in pan 3 minutes.
Build bowls: quinoa base, spinach, capsicum, chicken, avocado.
Drizzle with lemon and olive oil.
recipes.nutrition: Complete protein · All macros balanced · Nutrient-dense
nutritionSnapshot: Quinoa and chicken together provide all essential amino acids
Avocado and olive oil deliver heart-healthy monounsaturated fats
Iron from spinach absorbed better alongside lemon vitamin C
recipes.kidTip: Let kids build their own bowl from separate containers.
recipes.ageTip: Dice everything small for younger children. Suitable for all ages.

recipes.name: Grilled Salmon with Broccoli & Rice
steps: Cook rice per packet instructions.
Steam or roast broccoli until tender-crisp.
Season salmon with salt, pepper and lemon.
Grill salmon 4–5 minutes each side.
Toss broccoli in olive oil and garlic.
Serve salmon over rice with broccoli alongside.
recipes.nutrition: Omega-3 rich · High protein · Calcium from broccoli
nutritionSnapshot: Salmon provides DHA omega-3 for brain and eye development
Broccoli is one of the most nutrient-dense vegetables available
A perfectly balanced plate — protein, carbs, vegetables and healthy fats
recipes.kidTip: Tell kids broccoli are little trees — they love eating tiny trees!
recipes.ageTip: Flake salmon for younger children. Cut broccoli into small florets.

recipes.name: Meatballs with Potato Mash
steps: Preheat oven to 190°C.
Mix mince with egg, garlic, herbs, salt and pepper. Roll into balls.
Bake meatballs 25 minutes until cooked through.
Boil potatoes until tender, drain and mash with butter and warm milk.
Steam greens until tender.
Serve meatballs over creamy mash with greens.
recipes.nutrition: High protein · Comfort food done healthily · Calcium from mash
nutritionSnapshot: Meatballs are a universally loved children's meal
Homemade avoids additives in store-bought versions
Mashed potato provides slow-release energy and potassium
recipes.kidTip: Rolling meatballs is the perfect job for little helpers.
recipes.ageTip: Mash is the perfect texture for all ages. Cut meatballs for toddlers.

recipes.name: Chicken Soup with Noodles
steps: Sauté onion, garlic and ginger in oil until soft.
Add chicken, pour in stock, bring to boil.
Add carrots and celery, simmer 25 minutes.
Remove chicken, shred and return to pot.
Add noodles and cook 3–5 minutes until tender.
Season and serve with fresh herbs.
recipes.nutrition: High protein · Anti-inflammatory ginger · Gut-soothing
nutritionSnapshot: Homemade chicken broth has powerful anti-inflammatory properties
Ginger aids digestion and supports immunity
Noodle soup is one of the most comforting meals for unwell children
recipes.kidTip: Let kids slurp the noodles — fully encouraged here!
recipes.ageTip: Blend for babies. Cut noodles short for toddlers. Suitable for all ages.

recipes.name: Sardine Mediterranean Rice Bowl
steps: Warm rice if using leftovers.
Drain sardines and flake over rice.
Add tomatoes, cucumber and parsley.
Drizzle with olive oil and lemon juice.
Season and toss gently.
recipes.nutrition: Omega-3 rich · High protein · Gut-friendly Mediterranean ingredients
nutritionSnapshot: Sardines are one of the most omega-3 dense foods available
Mediterranean diet is consistently linked to better health outcomes in children
Ready in 15 minutes from pantry staples
recipes.kidTip: Introduce sardines gradually. Start with tinned in mild olive oil.
recipes.ageTip: Best for primary school age. Flake thoroughly and check for bones.

recipes.name: Chicken & Avocado Wrap
steps: Mash avocado with lemon juice, salt and pepper.
Spread avocado over wrap.
Layer spinach, chicken and cucumber.
Roll tightly and cut in half diagonally.
Wrap in baking paper for lunchboxes.
recipes.nutrition: High protein · Healthy fats · Easy lunchbox meal
nutritionSnapshot: Using leftover chicken makes this a zero-effort lunch
Avocado provides healthy fats to sustain energy through the afternoon
Wholegrain wrap gives slow-release carbohydrates
recipes.kidTip: Let kids help roll and wrap their own — they eat what they build.
recipes.ageTip: Cut into small rounds for toddlers. Remove wrap for gluten-free option.

recipes.name: Egg & Tuna Salad
steps: Boil eggs 8 minutes for hard boiled. Cool and peel.
Arrange salad leaves, cucumber and tomatoes.
Quarter eggs and place on salad.
Add tuna flaked over the top.
Drizzle with olive oil and lemon. Season.
recipes.nutrition: Very high protein · Omega-3 · Quick and nutritious
nutritionSnapshot: Two protein sources in one meal — eggs and tuna — for exceptional intake
Omega-3 from tuna supports brain function and concentration
Simple pantry ingredients ready in 15 minutes
recipes.kidTip: Let kids help peel the boiled eggs under running water.
recipes.ageTip: Best for older children. Chop everything finely for younger ones.

recipes.name: Pumpkin & Turkey Sausage Soup
steps: Sauté onion, garlic and ginger in coconut oil.
Brown sausage slices and set aside.
Add pumpkin and stock, bring to boil, simmer 20 minutes.
Blend soup until smooth.
Return sausage to pot and heat through.
Season and serve with wholegrain bread.
recipes.nutrition: Beta-carotene from pumpkin · Protein from turkey · Warming and filling
nutritionSnapshot: Pumpkin is rich in beta-carotene and vitamin A for immune health
Turkey is one of the leanest protein sources
Bright orange colour is naturally appealing to children
recipes.kidTip: Kids love the bright orange colour. Let them stir the pot.
recipes.ageTip: Blended smooth — perfect for all ages including toddlers.

recipes.name: Protein-Packed Lentil Soup
steps: Sauté onion and garlic in oil until soft.
Add cumin and turmeric, cook 1 minute until fragrant.
Add lentils, tomatoes and stock.
Bring to boil, simmer 25 minutes until lentils are soft.
Add lemon juice and season.
Blend half for creamier texture or leave chunky.
recipes.nutrition: Plant protein from lentils · Anti-inflammatory turmeric · High fibre
nutritionSnapshot: Lentils provide plant protein and iron — important for vegetarian families
Turmeric is a powerful anti-inflammatory — a golden spice for growing children
High fibre supports healthy gut bacteria
recipes.kidTip: Ask kids to count the lentils before they go in — great maths activity!
recipes.ageTip: Blend completely smooth for toddlers. Suitable for all ages.

recipes.name: Grilled Chicken Salad with Fruit
steps: Season and grill chicken 5–6 minutes each side. Slice.
Arrange salad leaves in a large bowl.
Add fruit, avocado and walnuts.
Top with sliced chicken.
Drizzle with olive oil and apple cider vinegar. Season.
recipes.nutrition: High protein · Antioxidants from berries · Omega-3 from walnuts
nutritionSnapshot: The combination of fruit and greens creates a rainbow of antioxidants
Walnuts provide omega-3 fatty acids and vitamin E
Apple cider vinegar supports healthy digestion
recipes.kidTip: A colourful salad is more appealing to children — let them arrange the fruit.
recipes.ageTip: Best for primary-aged children. Chop ingredients small for younger ones.

recipes.name: Raw Protein Bars
steps: Mix all ingredients together in a large bowl until well combined.
Press into a lined tray or roll into balls.
Freeze for 30 minutes until firm.
Cut into bars or serve as balls.
Store in freezer up to 1 month.
recipes.nutrition: High protein · No refined sugar · Healthy fats
nutritionSnapshot: Protein powder and almond butter together deliver excellent protein
Sweetened only with honey — no refined sugar
Make a batch and freeze — grab-and-go snacks for the whole week
recipes.kidTip: Kids love rolling the mixture into balls. Great school holiday activity.
recipes.ageTip: Best for primary school age. Check for nut allergies.

recipes.name: Devilled Eggs
steps: Hard boil eggs 10 minutes. Cool in cold water and peel.
Slice eggs in half lengthwise, scoop out yolks.
Mash yolks with avocado, mustard, lemon, salt and pepper.
Spoon or pipe mixture back into egg whites.
Dust with paprika and serve.
recipes.nutrition: Very high protein · Healthy fats from avocado · No carbs
nutritionSnapshot: Two eggs per person provides a complete high-protein snack
Avocado replaces mayo — much healthier fats
Naturally gluten-free and dairy-free
recipes.kidTip: Kids love spooning the filling back into the egg halves.
recipes.ageTip: Perfect for all ages. Excellent party food for children.

recipes.name: Simple Waffles
steps: Whisk all dry ingredients together.
Beat eggs, butter and milk together.
Combine wet and dry ingredients into a smooth batter.
Cook in waffle iron per manufacturer instructions.
Or cook as thick pancakes in a buttered pan.
Serve with fresh berries and a small drizzle of honey.
recipes.nutrition: Gluten-free · Protein from eggs · Healthy fats from almond flour
nutritionSnapshot: Rice and almond flour combination is naturally gluten-free
Eggs provide complete protein for growing children
No refined sugar — sweetened with erythritol
recipes.kidTip: Waffles are breakfast magic for kids. Let them pour the batter.
recipes.ageTip: Cut into strips for toddlers. Suitable for all ages.

recipes.name: Bedtime Raspberries with Yoghurt
steps: Spoon yoghurt into bowls.
Top with raspberries.
Drizzle honey.
Sprinkle chia seeds if using.
Serve immediately.
recipes.nutrition: Protein from yoghurt · Antioxidants from raspberries · Gut-friendly
nutritionSnapshot: Raspberries are rich in antioxidants and vitamin C
Greek yoghurt provides protein and probiotics for gut health
Light enough for a bedtime snack without disrupting sleep
recipes.kidTip: A calming bedtime ritual — same snack each night builds a healthy habit.
recipes.ageTip: Mash raspberries for toddlers. Suitable for all ages.

recipes.name: Fruit & Nut Yoghurt Salad
steps: Chop seasonal fruit into bite-sized pieces.
Combine fruit in a bowl.
Add dollops of yoghurt through the salad.
Scatter chopped nuts on top.
Drizzle with honey and dust with cinnamon.
recipes.nutrition: Antioxidants · Protein from yoghurt · Healthy fats from nuts
nutritionSnapshot: A rainbow of fruit provides diverse phytonutrients and vitamins
Nuts add healthy fats and protein for sustained energy
Yoghurt probiotics support immune function
recipes.kidTip: Make it a rainbow — encourage kids to pick one fruit of each colour.
recipes.ageTip: Chop nuts finely for under-5s. Remove whole nuts for toddlers.

recipes.name: Banana Berry Hotcakes
steps: Combine mashed banana, whisked eggs and protein powder/almond meal. Mix well.
Heat fry-pan over medium heat and melt coconut oil.
Spoon batter into pan to preferred size.
Cook 2–3 minutes until edges firm and it's stable enough to flip.
Flip and cook a further 1–2 minutes until golden brown.
Serve topped with coconut yoghurt and mixed berries.
recipes.nutrition: Gluten-free · Natural sweetness from banana · Protein-boosted
nutritionSnapshot: Banana provides natural sweetness and potassium without refined sugar
Eggs and protein powder give a protein-rich start to the day
Only 4 core ingredients — simple and quick on busy mornings
recipes.kidTip: Kids love flipping the hotcakes — set the heat low and supervise closely.
recipes.ageTip: Suitable for all ages. Cut into small pieces for toddlers. Naturally gluten-free.

recipes.name: Anti-Inflammatory Power Smoothie

ingredients.item: Plant-based protein powder or almond meal

steps: Add all ingredients to blender.
Blitz until smooth and creamy.
Pour into glass or travel bottle.
Sip slowly — 'chew' your smoothie to activate digestion.
recipes.nutrition: Anti-inflammatory turmeric · Healthy fats · Blood sugar regulating
nutritionSnapshot: Turmeric is one of the most powerful anti-inflammatory spices
Cinnamon helps regulate blood sugar — great for fussy eaters
Nut butter adds healthy fats for sustained morning energy
recipes.kidTip: Call it the golden superhero smoothie — the turmeric makes it glow!
recipes.ageTip: Best for preschool age and up. Ensure nut butter is appropriate for age.

recipes.name: Macadamia No-Grain-Ola

ingredients.item: Maple syrup or monk fruit syrup
Macadamia nuts, whole or roughly chopped

steps: Preheat oven to 160°C. Line a large baking tray with baking paper.
Combine melted coconut oil and maple syrup in a large bowl.
Add all other ingredients and mix until well coated.
Spread evenly over tray and bake 20 minutes until coconut turns golden.
Remove and cool completely before storing.
Store in airtight container up to 2 weeks.
Serve with berries and coconut yoghurt.
recipes.nutrition: Grain-free · Healthy fats · Natural sweetener
nutritionSnapshot: Macadamia nuts are the richest food source of heart-healthy monounsaturated fats
Grain-free and gluten-free — great for sensitive digestion
Packed with seeds for omega-3 and zinc
recipes.kidTip: Great for kids to help mix and spread on the tray.
recipes.ageTip: Chop nuts for under-5s. Whole macadamias are a choking risk for younger children.

recipes.name: Spiralled Salmon with Zucchini Noodles

ingredients.item: Toasted nuts or seeds to garnish

steps: Bring a saucepan of water to boil, reduce to simmer.
Poach salmon 4–6 minutes depending on preference. Remove and rest.
Combine zucchini noodles, rocket and chilli in a bowl.
Toss generously with olive oil and lemon juice.
Break salmon into chunks and serve on top.
Garnish with toasted nuts or seeds, season and serve.
recipes.nutrition: Omega-3 from salmon · Low carb zucchini noodles · Anti-inflammatory
nutritionSnapshot: Poaching is one of the gentlest cooking methods preserving omega-3
Zucchini noodles replace pasta for a light low-carb base
Rocket adds folate and vitamin K
recipes.kidTip: Kids love twirling zucchini noodles just like pasta!
recipes.ageTip: Best for primary school age. Skip chilli for younger children. Flake salmon carefully.

recipes.name: Gorgeous Green Chicken Cakes

ingredients.item: Kale, stems removed and roughly chopped

steps: Combine chicken mince, kale, sesame seeds, garlic powder, onion powder, salt and pepper.
Mix well with hands until evenly combined.
Shape into patties of preferred size.
Heat coconut oil in fry pan over medium heat.
Cook patties 4–5 minutes each side until golden and cooked through.
Mash avocado with lemon juice and salt.
Serve patties on dressed greens with smashed avocado.
recipes.nutrition: High protein · Kale boosts nutrients · Healthy fats from avocado
nutritionSnapshot: Kale is one of the most nutrient-dense vegetables available
Chicken mince makes patties that are easy for children to eat
Avocado provides healthy monounsaturated fats and vitamin E
recipes.kidTip: Kids can help shape the patties — the kale gives them a fun green colour!
recipes.ageTip: Make small patties for younger children. Remove chilli for toddlers.

recipes.name: Pumpkin Patties with Avo & Salad

ingredients.item: Extra virgin olive oil and lemon juice

steps: Preheat oven to 180°C. Drizzle coconut oil over pumpkin, bake 10–15 minutes until soft.
Transfer cooked pumpkin to bowl, mash and combine with egg, flours and spices. Season.
Heat coconut oil in fry pan over medium heat.
Shape into patties and cook until stable, golden on both sides.
Serve alongside smashed avocado, cherry tomatoes and dressed salad.
recipes.nutrition: Vegetarian · Beta-carotene from pumpkin · Gluten-free
nutritionSnapshot: Pumpkin provides beta-carotene which converts to vitamin A
Almond meal adds healthy fats and keeps patties gluten-free
Great make-ahead recipe — enjoy hot or cold
recipes.kidTip: The orange colour makes these fun — kids love the vibrant look on their plate.
recipes.ageTip: Soft texture suitable for all ages. Skip chilli flakes for young children.

recipes.name: Fantastic Chicken Fajitas (Lettuce Wraps)
steps: Coat chopped chicken with dry spices.
Cook in coconut oil over medium heat 6–8 minutes until cooked through.
Dress red cabbage, avocado and greens with olive oil and lemon juice.
Use large lettuce leaves as wraps.
Fill with chicken and trimmings, season and enjoy.
recipes.nutrition: High protein · Anti-inflammatory spices · Grain-free
nutritionSnapshot: Turmeric and cumin provide powerful anti-inflammatory benefits
Using lettuce as wraps eliminates grains while adding hydrating crunch
A rainbow of colours in one plate means diverse phytonutrients
recipes.kidTip: Kids love assembling their own lettuce wraps — it's interactive and fun.
recipes.ageTip: Skip chilli for younger children. Cut chicken small for toddlers.

recipes.name: Spicy Udon Noodle Curry (Zucchini Noodles)
steps: Heat coconut oil in pan, sauté garlic, onion 2–3 minutes.
Add mince and brown on all sides.
Add dry spices, coconut milk and broth. Stir and simmer until sauce thickens.
Cut zucchini into thick noodles with a knife or mandolin.
Add zucchini noodles to mince mixture just before serving, stir to soften.
Transfer to bowl and garnish with fresh coriander.
recipes.nutrition: High protein · Anti-inflammatory turmeric · Grain-free noodles
nutritionSnapshot: Zucchini noodles replace pasta — grain-free and very low carb
Bone broth base provides collagen and supports gut health
Turmeric and garam masala are powerfully anti-inflammatory
recipes.kidTip: For younger children reduce spices significantly — make a mild version.
recipes.ageTip: Best for primary school age as written. Reduce all spices for toddlers.

recipes.name: Pumpkin & Sundried Tomato Veggie Bread
steps: Preheat oven to 180°C. Grease and line a loaf tin.
Sauté onion and garlic in coconut oil 3–4 minutes until softened.
In a large bowl, whisk eggs and coconut cream.
Add almond meal and baking powder, mix well.
Fold in pumpkin, spinach, sundried tomatoes and onion mixture. Season.
Pour into loaf tin and bake 30–40 minutes until golden and a skewer comes out clean.
Cool on a wire rack. Store fridge 5 days or slice and freeze up to 3 months.
recipes.nutrition: Grain-free · High protein from eggs · Hidden vegetables
nutritionSnapshot: Eight eggs provide exceptional protein in every slice
Pumpkin and spinach hidden inside — kids eat vegetables without knowing
Almond meal base is naturally gluten-free and high in healthy fats
recipes.kidTip: Kids can help grate the pumpkin — a great sensory activity.
recipes.ageTip: Suitable for all ages. Cut thin slices for toddlers.

recipes.name: Paleo Brookie (Brownie-Cookie)
steps: Preheat oven to 180°C. Line a 20cm square baking tin.
Place all ingredients except raspberries in a bowl and mix to form a batter.
Spread evenly over prepared tin.
Dot raspberries over the surface.
Bake 25–30 minutes until top is firm and crunchy.
Cool in tin before cutting into 12 pieces.
Store in airtight container up to 7 days.
recipes.nutrition: Gluten-free · No refined sugar · Rich in antioxidants from cacao
nutritionSnapshot: Raw cacao is rich in antioxidants and magnesium
Sweetened with coconut sugar or monkfruit — much lower GI than refined sugar
Almond meal provides healthy fats and is naturally grain-free
recipes.kidTip: Kids love pressing the raspberries into the batter before baking.
recipes.ageTip: Suitable for all ages. A healthier treat option for lunchboxes.

recipes.name: Breakfast Casserole with Sausages
steps: Preheat oven to 190°C / 375°F.
Cook sausage in skillet, crumbling as it cooks. Transfer to large bowl.
Cook onion, garlic and capsicum in same skillet 4–5 minutes. Add to bowl.
Cook sweet potatoes in skillet 8 minutes, season, add to bowl.
Pour sausage and sweet potato mixture into baking dish.
Whisk eggs with milk, salt and pepper. Pour over mixture.
Bake 20 minutes. Serve with green onions on top.
recipes.nutrition: Very high protein · Slow-release carbs from sweet potato · Complete meal
nutritionSnapshot: Eight eggs and sausages create an exceptionally protein-rich breakfast
Sweet potato provides slow-release energy to sustain children all morning
Make ahead and reheat — a great meal prep breakfast
recipes.kidTip: Kids can help crack the eggs and whisk them — a classic kitchen job.
recipes.ageTip: Soft texture suitable for all ages. Cut into small squares for toddlers.

recipes.name: Scrambled Eggs with Smoked Salmon
steps: Whisk eggs, coconut milk and chives together. Season to taste.
Melt cooking fat in skillet over medium-low heat.
Add eggs and scramble gently while cooking.
When eggs start to settle, add smoked salmon.
Cook 1–2 more minutes until just set.
Serve topped with more fresh chives.
recipes.nutrition: Omega-3 from salmon · High protein · Healthy fats
nutritionSnapshot: Combining eggs and salmon delivers both omega-3 and complete protein
Coconut milk adds creaminess without dairy
One of the fastest high-protein breakfasts possible
recipes.kidTip: The pink of the salmon against the yellow eggs makes a beautiful plate.
recipes.ageTip: Suitable for all ages. Check salmon is not too salty for toddlers.

recipes.name: Greek-Style Meatballs
steps: Preheat oven to 175°C / 350°F.
Combine all ingredients in a bowl and mix well with hands.
Form into equally-sized meatballs.
Place on baking sheet and bake 20–25 minutes.
Serve with roasted vegetables or a simple salad.
recipes.nutrition: High protein · Iron-rich · Herb-packed
nutritionSnapshot: Mint and oregano are the classic Greek flavour combination
Lamb provides iron and zinc in highly bioavailable forms
Baked not fried — much healthier than traditional fried meatballs
recipes.kidTip: Rolling meatballs is endlessly satisfying for children of all ages.
recipes.ageTip: Cut in half for younger children. Very soft when baked — suitable for all ages.

recipes.name: Ham & Butternut Squash Breakfast Hash
steps: Melt cooking fat in skillet over medium-high heat.
Cook onion and garlic 2–3 minutes.
Add butternut squash and leek, cook 5–7 minutes until tender.
Add ham and apple, cook until warm.
Season with cinnamon, paprika, salt and pepper.
Cook another 2 minutes and serve warm.
recipes.nutrition: Protein from ham · Beta-carotene from squash · Apple adds natural sweetness
nutritionSnapshot: Apple and cinnamon create natural sweetness that children love
Butternut squash provides beta-carotene for immune health
A satisfying savoury breakfast that keeps children full until lunch
recipes.kidTip: The sweet apple and cinnamon make this a breakfast kids will ask for again.
recipes.ageTip: Naturally soft textures — suitable for all ages. Dice small for toddlers.

recipes.name: Chicken Pad See Ew
steps: Heat cooking fat in wok or large skillet over medium-high heat.
Sauté garlic and ginger 2–3 minutes.
Add chicken and cook 5 minutes.
Add broccoli, coconut aminos and honey. Cook until broccoli softens.
Add carrot and green onions, cook 5 more minutes.
Push everything to perimeter of wok, scramble eggs in the centre.
Mix everything together and serve.
recipes.nutrition: High protein · Vitamin C from broccoli · Grain-free
nutritionSnapshot: Coconut aminos replace soy sauce — lower sodium and gluten-free
Broccoli provides vitamin C which boosts iron absorption from chicken
A complete one-wok meal ready in under 30 minutes
recipes.kidTip: Kids love the scrambled egg mixed through — ask them to spot the eggs!
recipes.ageTip: Cut all ingredients very small for toddlers. Reduce coconut aminos to reduce sodium.

recipes.name: Garlic Roasted Cod
steps: Preheat oven to 220°C / 425°F.
Combine ghee, parsley, garlic, mustard and lemon juice. Season.
Season cod fillets with salt and pepper.
Heat cooking fat in ovenproof skillet, cook fillets 3–4 minutes each side.
Spread ghee mixture over each fillet.
Transfer to oven and bake 5–10 minutes until cooked through.
Serve with lemon wedges and green beans.
recipes.nutrition: High lean protein · Omega-3 · Simple and clean
nutritionSnapshot: Cod is one of the mildest white fish — great for children new to seafood
Garlic herb crust adds flavour without breading
A restaurant-quality dinner ready in under 30 minutes
recipes.kidTip: The garlic butter topping smells incredible — it gets kids to the table fast!
recipes.ageTip: Remove all bones carefully. Mild flavour suitable for all ages.

recipes.name: Maple Braised Chuck Roast with Caramelised Vegetables
steps: Preheat oven to 175°C / 350°F.
Combine stock, maple syrup, balsamic, coconut aminos, garlic and onion in a bowl.
Brown meat on all sides in Dutch oven, pour sauce over top.
Cook in oven 2.5 hours.
Toss vegetables in olive oil, honey and thyme on a baking sheet.
Roast vegetables last 30–40 minutes of beef cooking time.
Serve meat with caramelised vegetables and pan juices.
recipes.nutrition: Very high protein · Rich iron · Slow-release carbs from sweet potato
nutritionSnapshot: Slow roasting makes the meat fall-apart tender — perfect for all ages
Sweet potato caramelises beautifully and is naturally loved by children
Balsamic and coconut aminos create a deep rich flavour without gluten
recipes.kidTip: The aroma while this cooks is incredible — kids will be asking when dinner is ready!
recipes.ageTip: Meat falls apart — perfect for all ages including toddlers.

recipes.name: Apple & Onion Scrambled Eggs
steps: Melt cooking fat in skillet over medium heat.
Cook onion and celery 2–3 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add apple and cook another 1–2 minutes.
Pour beaten eggs over mixture.
Stir gently until slightly underdone. Remove from heat.
Rest 1–2 minutes, season and serve topped with chives.
recipes.nutrition: High protein · Apple adds natural sweetness · Quick and filling
nutritionSnapshot: Apple adds natural sweetness that children enjoy in savoury dishes
Eight eggs across 4 serves provides exceptional protein
Ready in under 10 minutes — perfect busy morning breakfast
recipes.kidTip: The apple makes these scrambled eggs surprisingly sweet — kids love the twist!
recipes.ageTip: Suitable for all ages. The natural sweetness from apple is well received by toddlers.

recipes.name: Beef & Winter Vegetable Soup
steps: Brown beef on each side in cooking fat. Set aside.
Cook onion and garlic until fragrant 5 minutes.
Add half the stock to deglaze the pan.
Add all vegetables and cook stirring 10 minutes.
Return beef, add remaining stock and cover.
Simmer 40–45 minutes until meat is cooked through.
Adjust seasoning, sprinkle parsley and serve.
recipes.nutrition: Very high iron · Rich in B vitamins · Complete one-pot meal
nutritionSnapshot: Beef provides highly bioavailable iron, zinc and B12
Cauliflower rice adds nutrients without adding extra carbs
A deeply nourishing winter meal the whole family will love
recipes.kidTip: The rich broth is incredibly nourishing — great when children are under the weather.
recipes.ageTip: Blend smooth for toddlers or serve with very soft tender beef chunks.

recipes.name: Breakfast Stuffed Capsicums
steps: Preheat oven to 190°C / 375°F.
Sauté onion until soft 4 minutes, add mushrooms and tomatoes 2 minutes.
Add spinach and cook until wilted. Season with garlic powder, salt and pepper.
Divide vegetable mixture equally among capsicum halves.
Pour beaten eggs over each capsicum half.
Bake on a tray 40 minutes until eggs are set.
Serve immediately.
recipes.nutrition: High protein · Packed with vegetables · Gluten-free
nutritionSnapshot: Each capsicum half is a self-contained balanced breakfast
Bell pepper provides more vitamin C than an orange
A visually striking breakfast that children find exciting
recipes.kidTip: Let kids choose their own capsicum colour — red, yellow or green.
recipes.ageTip: Best for preschool age and up. The capsicum wall holds everything in place nicely.

recipes.name: Pistachio-Crusted Salmon
steps: Preheat oven to 190°C / 375°F.
Combine pistachios, lemon juice, mustard, honey and dill. Season.
Press pistachio mixture firmly on top of each salmon fillet.
Place in oven and bake uncovered 15–20 minutes until cooked through.
Rest 2–4 minutes before serving.
recipes.nutrition: Omega-3 rich · Healthy fats from pistachios · Impressive and easy
nutritionSnapshot: Pistachios are rich in protein, healthy fats and antioxidants
Wild salmon provides the highest omega-3 content
The crust makes this feel like a restaurant meal — great for entertaining
recipes.kidTip: Kids can help press the pistachio crust onto the salmon fillets.
recipes.ageTip: Check for nut allergies. Chop pistachios very finely. Remove all bones carefully.

recipes.name: Spicy Pork Chili
steps: Preheat oven to 175°C / 350°F.
Warm oil, sauté garlic, onions 3–5 minutes.
Add capsicum, tomatoes, paprika, cumin, oregano, salt and pepper.
Place pork shoulder in pot, add vinegar, cover with water.
Bring to boil, cover and place in oven 3 hours.
Meat is done when it breaks apart easily with a fork.
Serve over rice or with roasted sweet potato.
recipes.nutrition: Very high protein from pork · Lycopene from tomatoes · Rich and satisfying
nutritionSnapshot: Pork shoulder slow-cooked becomes very tender and easy to eat
Lycopene in tomatoes supports cardiovascular health
The long slow cook develops incredibly deep flavour
recipes.kidTip: Use leftovers in school lunchbox wraps the next day.
recipes.ageTip: Reduce or omit chilli for younger children. Shred meat very finely for toddlers.

recipes.name: Cherry Tomato & Basil Breakfast Quiche
steps: Preheat oven to 175°C / 350°F.
Beat eggs with garlic and fresh basil. Season.
Pour into small baking dish.
Place cherry tomatoes cut-side up in the egg mixture.
Bake 20 minutes until set.
Serve with extra fresh basil.
recipes.nutrition: High protein · Lycopene from tomatoes · Simple and clean
nutritionSnapshot: Only 4 ingredients — as simple and clean as it gets
Cherry tomatoes are rich in lycopene and vitamin C
Fresh basil adds flavour and antioxidants
recipes.kidTip: Let kids place the cherry tomatoes into the egg mixture cut-side up.
recipes.ageTip: Suitable for all ages. Soft texture easy for toddlers.

recipes.name: Ground Beef & Cabbage Skillet
steps: Melt cooking fat in large skillet over medium-high heat.
Cook garlic and onion until fragrant and soft.
Brown beef mince until no longer pink.
Add cabbage, tomatoes and tomato sauce. Season.
Cover and simmer 25 minutes until cabbage is very soft.
Serve on its own or over rice.
recipes.nutrition: High protein · Hidden cabbage · One-pan simplicity
nutritionSnapshot: Cabbage is rich in vitamin C and compounds that support gut health
One pan means less washing up — a win for busy families
The tomato sauce makes the cabbage disappear — kids rarely notice it
recipes.kidTip: Cabbage hides beautifully in tomato sauce — most children eat it without noticing.
recipes.ageTip: Cabbage becomes very soft — suitable for all ages. Serve over rice for toddlers.

recipes.name: Denver-Style Egg Bake
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C / 400°F. Line a 23x33cm baking dish with baking paper.
Sauté onion and capsicum in oil 3 minutes until soft.
Whisk eggs with almond milk, salt and pepper in a large bowl.
Arrange ham and sautéed vegetables in baking dish.
Pour egg mixture over the top.
Bake 30–35 minutes until middle is set and firm to touch.
Cool before cutting and serving — great hot or cold.
recipes.nutrition: Very high protein · Packed with vegetables · Great batch cook
nutritionSnapshot: 12 eggs across 4 serves delivers exceptional protein for the whole family
Ham adds extra protein with minimal extra effort
Bakes hands-free — pop it in and come back 35 minutes later
recipes.kidTip: Kids can help crack the eggs and whisk them with the milk.
recipes.ageTip: Cut into small squares for toddlers. Soft texture suitable for all ages.

recipes.name: Kitchen Sink Scrambler
steps: Whisk eggs in a bowl and set aside.
Cook bacon in skillet over medium-high heat 3 minutes until fat releases.
Add onion, mushrooms and capsicum, sauté 3 minutes until bacon is brown.
Add spinach and stir 30 seconds until partially wilted.
Pour in eggs and stir 2 minutes until fluffy and scrambled.
Season and serve immediately.
recipes.nutrition: High protein from eggs and bacon · Iron from spinach · No carbs
nutritionSnapshot: Bacon fat adds rich flavour while providing healthy saturated fats
Spinach and capsicum add iron and vitamin C
Ready in 15 minutes — a genuinely quick high-protein breakfast
recipes.kidTip: Let kids choose what goes in from a selection of veggies — they eat what they picked.
recipes.ageTip: Reduce bacon quantity for younger children. Chop everything very small for toddlers.

recipes.name: Sausage & Spinach Egg Muffins
steps: Preheat oven to 175°C / 350°F. Grease a 12-hole muffin tin.
Whisk eggs and coconut milk together in a large bowl.
Fold in sausage, spinach, scallions and garlic.
Season with salt and pepper.
Fill each muffin cup two-thirds full.
Bake 30–35 minutes until middle is set and no longer runny.
Cool in pan before removing. Store up to 4 days in fridge.
recipes.nutrition: High protein · Gluten-free · Portable lunchbox food
nutritionSnapshot: Each muffin provides protein from both eggs and sausage
Coconut milk makes these dairy-free and adds healthy fats
Make 12 on Sunday — breakfast or lunchbox sorted all week
recipes.kidTip: Kids can spoon the mixture into the muffin tin holes.
recipes.ageTip: Perfect for all ages. Soft texture easy for toddlers. Cut in half if needed.

recipes.name: Sweet Potato Hash Browns
steps: Wrap shredded sweet potato in a clean towel and squeeze out excess moisture.
Heat coconut oil in a cast iron skillet over medium-high heat.
Combine sweet potato with arrowroot flour, salt and pepper.
Scoop 2 tablespoons at a time into the skillet and flatten with a spatula.
Fry 2–3 minutes until golden and crispy, flip, fry 2–3 more minutes.
Remove to a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
recipes.nutrition: Beta-carotene from sweet potato · Gluten-free · Crispy and delicious
nutritionSnapshot: Sweet potato provides beta-carotene and vitamin A
Arrowroot flour keeps these gluten-free and gives crispy edges
A healthier alternative to regular hash browns — kids love them
recipes.kidTip: Kids love the crispy texture. Serve with eggs for a complete breakfast.
recipes.ageTip: Suitable for all ages. Cut small for toddlers.

recipes.name: Fresh Herbed Breakfast Sausages
steps: Heat coconut oil in cast iron skillet over medium-high heat.
Mix pork with thyme, sage, salt, garlic and onion powder.
Divide into 8–10 balls and press each into a patty.
Fry 5 minutes per side until outer layer is crispy and cooked through (70°C / 160°F).
Serve immediately alongside eggs or as part of a full breakfast.
recipes.nutrition: High protein · No additives or nitrates · Clean ingredients
nutritionSnapshot: Homemade sausages contain none of the additives in store-bought versions
Pork provides high protein, B vitamins and zinc
Can be batch made and frozen — reheat in minutes
recipes.kidTip: Kids can help press the patties into shape — a satisfying hands-on job.
recipes.ageTip: Ensure fully cooked through. Cut small for toddlers.

recipes.name: Avocado Egg Tankers
steps: Preheat oven to 220°C / 425°F.
Remove 1–2 tablespoons of avocado flesh from each half to create a well.
Place each avocado half in a muffin tin hole to prevent tipping.
Crack 1 egg into each avocado half.
Season with salt and pepper.
Bake 20 minutes until egg white is completely set.
Let stand 5 minutes, top with chives or bacon bits if desired.
recipes.nutrition: Healthy fats from avocado · Protein from egg · Only 2 ingredients
nutritionSnapshot: Avocado and egg together provide one of the most complete nutrient combinations
Healthy monounsaturated fats from avocado support brain development
Only 2 core ingredients — the simplest high-nutrition breakfast possible
recipes.kidTip: Kids find the egg baked inside the avocado absolutely fascinating.
recipes.ageTip: Best for preschool age and up. Remove the avocado skin carefully before serving.

recipes.name: Apple-Cinnamon Porridge (Grain-Free)
steps: Combine all ingredients in a medium saucepan over medium heat.
Bring to a gentle boil, then cover and simmer 20 minutes.
Stir occasionally until porridge is tender and fragrant.
Serve immediately topped with extra apple or a drizzle of maple syrup.
recipes.nutrition: Grain-free · Dairy-free · Natural sweetness from apple
nutritionSnapshot: Cauliflower rice and coconut make a grain-free alternative to traditional oats
Apple provides natural sweetness and fibre without any added sugar
Cinnamon helps regulate blood sugar — ideal for growing children
recipes.kidTip: The apple and cinnamon smell incredible while cooking — builds great excitement.
recipes.ageTip: Suitable for all ages including babies. A perfect first solid food base.

recipes.name: Garden-Fresh Tomato & Basil Soup
steps: Heat oil in large pot, sauté onion and garlic 3 minutes.
Add tomatoes and stock, bring to boil.
Cover and boil 10 minutes until tomatoes soften.
Transfer to blender, add tomato paste, coconut milk, salt and pepper.
Blend until smooth.
Fold in fresh basil and serve.
recipes.nutrition: Lycopene from tomatoes · Anti-inflammatory basil · Dairy-free cream
nutritionSnapshot: Tomatoes are rich in lycopene — a powerful antioxidant for heart health
Coconut milk adds creaminess without dairy
