A classic soup children universally love
recipes.kidTip: Kids love dipping sourdough bread soldiers into this — pair together for a full meal.
recipes.ageTip: Blended smooth — suitable for all ages including toddlers.

recipes.name: Southwest Chili
steps: Brown beef mince and onion in a large skillet over medium-high heat 5 minutes.
Add tomato sauce, diced tomatoes and all spices. Stir.
Bring to a gentle boil.
Cover and cook on low heat 20 minutes until thick and fragrant.
Serve over cauliflower rice or with sweet potato wedges.
recipes.nutrition: High protein · Lycopene from tomatoes · Warming and filling
nutritionSnapshot: Beef mince provides iron and zinc in their most bioavailable forms
Cumin and paprika add depth of flavour and anti-inflammatory benefits
Great batch cook — tastes even better the next day
recipes.kidTip: Reduce chilli powder by half for younger children. Serve with sour cream if they eat dairy.
recipes.ageTip: Go easy on chilli for under-5s. Serve over rice for toddlers.

recipes.name: Creamy Cauliflower Soup
steps: Cook bacon in large pot over medium-high heat until crispy. Remove and set aside.
In bacon fat, sauté onion and garlic 3 minutes until translucent.
Add coconut milk, broth, cauliflower, salt and pepper. Bring to boil.
Cover and simmer 15 minutes until cauliflower is fork-tender.
Blend until smooth and creamy.
Serve topped with crispy bacon bits and fresh chives.
recipes.nutrition: Creamy without dairy · Vitamin C from cauliflower · Protein from bacon
nutritionSnapshot: Cauliflower is rich in vitamin C and sulphur compounds supporting detoxification
Coconut milk makes this beautifully creamy without any dairy
The bacon topping adds crunch and protein — kids love it
recipes.kidTip: Let kids sprinkle the bacon bits and chives on top — they eat what they garnish.
recipes.ageTip: Blend completely smooth for toddlers. Suitable for all ages.

recipes.name: Classic Chicken Soup
steps: Heat olive oil in large pot over medium heat.
Sauté onion, carrots, celery and garlic with salt and pepper 5 minutes.
Add bone broth and bring to gentle boil.
Cover and simmer 15 minutes.
Add shredded chicken and parsley.
Season to taste and serve.
recipes.nutrition: Anti-inflammatory bone broth · Very high protein · Gut-healing
nutritionSnapshot: Chicken bone broth contains collagen and gelatin that support gut lining repair
Proven anti-inflammatory properties — the original healing food
One of the best meals when children are sick or run-down
recipes.kidTip: Add rice noodles or cauliflower rice for a heartier meal.
recipes.ageTip: Blend smooth for babies. Suitable for all ages. Perfect for sick children.

recipes.name: Perfectly Marinated Salmon
steps: Preheat oven to 220°C / 425°F. Line a baking sheet.
Combine olive oil, coconut aminos, honey, parsley, garlic, lemon, salt and pepper.
Coat salmon in the marinade. Leave 10 minutes at room temperature.
Place on baking sheet and bake 15 minutes until salmon has a slightly translucent pink centre.
Rest 5 minutes before serving.
recipes.nutrition: Omega-3 rich · Honey-glazed · Simple and impressive
nutritionSnapshot: Wild salmon provides the highest omega-3 DHA content for brain development
Coconut aminos replace soy sauce — gluten-free and lower sodium
The honey-garlic marinade creates a beautiful glaze kids love
recipes.kidTip: The sweet glaze makes this one of the most kid-friendly salmon dishes.
recipes.ageTip: Check carefully for bones. Flake onto plate for younger children.

recipes.name: Baked Salmon Patties
steps: Preheat oven to 190°C / 375°F. Line a baking sheet.
Drain salmon thoroughly and flake into a bowl removing any bones.
Mix in eggs, almond flour, onion, herbs, lemon, garlic powder, salt and pepper.
Form into 8 patties and place on baking sheet.
Bake 20–25 minutes, flipping halfway, until golden.
Serve with salad, sweet potato or in lunchboxes cold.
recipes.nutrition: Omega-3 rich · High protein · Gluten-free · Budget-friendly
nutritionSnapshot: Tinned salmon is one of the most affordable and convenient omega-3 sources
Almond flour binding makes these naturally gluten-free
Great hot or cold — perfect for lunchboxes the next day
recipes.kidTip: Involve kids in patty-shaping — it increases how much they eat.
recipes.ageTip: Soft texture suitable for all ages. Always check for any remaining bones.

recipes.name: Orange Chicken with Broccoli
steps: Mix orange juice, coconut aminos, honey, garlic and ginger in a small bowl.
Whisk in arrowroot flour until smooth. Set sauce aside.
Heat coconut oil in wok or large pan over high heat.
Cook chicken in batches until golden — about 5 minutes. Remove.
Add broccoli to pan, stir-fry 3 minutes.
Return chicken, pour sauce over and toss until thickened and glossy.
Serve over cauliflower rice or regular rice, garnish with sesame seeds.
recipes.nutrition: High protein · Vitamin C from orange and broccoli · Natural sweetener
nutritionSnapshot: Orange juice provides vitamin C which boosts iron absorption from chicken
Broccoli adds calcium, vitamin K and cancer-protective compounds
Sweetened with honey instead of refined sugar — a much healthier takeaway alternative
recipes.kidTip: The sweet orange glaze makes this one of the most popular family chicken dishes.
recipes.ageTip: Cut chicken very small for toddlers. Broccoli becomes soft when stir-fried.

recipes.name: Crispy Baked Chicken Thighs
steps: Preheat oven to 220°C / 425°F.
Pat chicken thighs completely dry with paper towel — key to crispy skin.
Brush with oil and season generously with all spices.
Place skin-side up on a wire rack over a baking tray.
Bake 35–40 minutes until skin is deeply golden and crispy.
Internal temperature should reach 75°C / 165°F.
Rest 5 minutes before serving.
recipes.nutrition: High protein · Iron-rich · Crispy skin with minimal effort
nutritionSnapshot: Chicken thighs contain more iron and zinc than breast meat
The dry rub creates an incredible crust with zero additives
One of the simplest high-protein family meals to prepare
recipes.kidTip: Kids love eating chicken on the bone — it's satisfying and interactive.
recipes.ageTip: Remove from bone for toddlers. Ensure fully cooked through.

recipes.name: Baja Chicken Fajitas
steps: Season chicken strips with cumin, paprika, garlic powder, salt and pepper.
Heat coconut oil in a large pan over high heat.
Cook chicken 5–6 minutes until golden. Remove and set aside.
Add capsicum and onion to same pan, cook 4 minutes until slightly charred.
Return chicken to pan, squeeze lime juice over, toss together.
Serve in large lettuce leaves topped with sliced avocado.
recipes.nutrition: High protein · Vitamin C from capsicum · Grain-free wraps
nutritionSnapshot: Capsicum contains more vitamin C than oranges
Using lettuce leaves as wraps eliminates grains and adds hydrating crunch
A complete meal in one pan — protein, fat and vegetables all together
recipes.kidTip: Kids love building their own fajita wraps — totally interactive and fun.
recipes.ageTip: Cut chicken small for toddlers. Skip chilli for younger children.

recipes.name: Slow Cooker Beef Roast with Gravy
steps: Season beef generously with salt and pepper.
Brown beef on all sides in coconut oil — optional but adds depth.
Place all ingredients in slow cooker.
Cook on low 7–8 hours or high 4–5 hours until beef falls apart.
Remove beef and shred with two forks.
Whisk arrowroot into the cooking liquid to make gravy.
Serve beef with gravy over mashed sweet potato or roasted vegetables.
recipes.nutrition: Very high protein · Iron and B12 rich · Collagen from slow cooking
nutritionSnapshot: Slow cooking creates collagen from the connective tissue — excellent for gut health
Beef provides iron and B12 in their most bioavailable forms
Add everything in the morning — an effortless family dinner waits when you get home
recipes.kidTip: The falling-apart beef is universally loved by children — tender and easy to chew.
recipes.ageTip: Perfectly tender for all ages including toddlers. Serve over mash for little ones.

recipes.name: Beef & Broccoli Stir-Fry

ingredients.item: Beef sirloin or rump, thinly sliced
Rice or cauliflower rice to serve

steps: Whisk coconut aminos, honey, garlic, ginger and arrowroot together. Set aside.
Heat coconut oil in wok on very high heat.
Cook beef in batches 2–3 minutes until browned. Remove.
Add broccoli to wok, stir-fry 3 minutes.
Return beef, add sauce, toss until thick and glossy — about 2 minutes.
Drizzle sesame oil, serve over rice or cauliflower rice.
recipes.nutrition: Iron from beef and broccoli · Quick · A classic combination
nutritionSnapshot: Beef and broccoli together provide exceptional iron — much better absorbed than plant sources alone
Coconut aminos replace soy sauce with a gluten-free lower-sodium alternative
Ready in under 20 minutes — faster than ordering takeaway
recipes.kidTip: A healthier version of Chinese takeaway — kids absolutely love it.
recipes.ageTip: Cut beef very small for toddlers. Reduce coconut aminos for younger children.

recipes.name: Swedish Meatballs
steps: Preheat oven to 190°C. Mix beef, pork, egg and spices together.
Roll into 20 small meatballs and bake 20–25 minutes.
Meanwhile make gravy: heat stock in a saucepan, whisk in arrowroot until thick.
Stir in coconut milk and simmer 5 minutes.
Add baked meatballs to gravy and serve over mashed sweet potato.
recipes.nutrition: High protein · Two-meat blend for extra flavour · Dairy-free gravy
nutritionSnapshot: Combining beef and pork creates exceptionally flavourful meatballs
The coconut milk gravy is dairy-free and incredibly creamy
A universally beloved children's meal — meatballs work every time
recipes.kidTip: Kids love rolling meatballs — set them up as the sous chef.
recipes.ageTip: Cut meatballs in half for younger children. Gravy adds moisture making them easy to chew.

recipes.name: Sunrise Breakfast Hash

ingredients.item: Sweet potato, peeled and diced small

steps: Heat coconut oil in cast iron skillet over medium-high heat.
Cook sweet potato and onion 8 minutes until sweet potato is tender.
Transfer to a bowl.
Brown beef mince in same skillet 5 minutes. Drain excess fat.
Add garlic powder, onion powder and salt to beef.
Return vegetables to skillet, stir to combine and serve.
recipes.nutrition: High protein · Beta-carotene from sweet potato · Filling and balanced
nutritionSnapshot: Sweet potato provides beta-carotene and slow-release energy to power the morning
Beef mince gives iron and zinc for immune function and growth
A complete breakfast protein + carbs + vegetables in one pan
recipes.kidTip: Who said breakfast can't be like dinner? Kids love this hearty hash.
recipes.ageTip: Dice everything very small for toddlers. Suitable for all ages.

recipes.name: Chocolate Chip Coconut Balls

ingredients.item: Paleo chocolate chips or chopped dark chocolate

steps: Combine coconut, almond butter, maple syrup, cacao, coconut oil, vanilla and salt.
Mix thoroughly until a sticky dough forms.
Fold in chocolate chips.
Roll into 16 balls using your hands.
Place on a lined tray and refrigerate 30 minutes until firm.
Store in airtight container in fridge up to 2 weeks or freeze.
recipes.nutrition: No refined sugar · Healthy fats · Natural sweetener
nutritionSnapshot: Almond butter and coconut provide healthy fats for sustained energy
Sweetened only with maple syrup — no refined sugar
Freezes beautifully — make a big batch and grab from freezer as needed
recipes.kidTip: Kids love rolling these — it's the perfect no-bake cooking activity.
recipes.ageTip: Suitable for all ages. Check for nut allergies. Cut in half for toddlers.

recipes.name: Paleo Chocolate Muffins
steps: Preheat oven to 175°C / 350°F. Line a muffin tin.
Combine almond flour, cacao, baking soda and salt in a bowl.
Whisk eggs, maple syrup, coconut oil and vanilla in another bowl.
Fold wet into dry until just combined.
Stir in chocolate chips.
Fill muffin cups two-thirds full.
Bake 20–22 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean.
Cool completely before serving.
recipes.nutrition: Gluten-free · No refined sugar · Rich antioxidants from cacao
nutritionSnapshot: Almond flour provides healthy fats and is naturally gluten-free
Raw cacao is rich in magnesium and antioxidants
Sweetened with maple syrup — much lower glycemic impact than refined sugar
recipes.kidTip: Kids can help scoop batter into muffin cases — they feel very involved.
recipes.ageTip: Suitable for all ages. Check for nut allergies.

recipes.name: Seasoned Carrot Fries

ingredients.item: Carrots, peeled and cut into thin sticks

steps: Preheat oven to 210°C / 425°F.
Cut carrots into thin, uniform sticks — the thinner the crispier.
Toss with olive oil, garlic powder, paprika, salt and pepper.
Spread in a single layer on a baking tray — do not crowd.
Roast 25–30 minutes, turning halfway, until edges are caramelised.
Serve immediately — best eaten hot.
recipes.nutrition: Beta-carotene · Naturally sweet · Much healthier than potato fries
nutritionSnapshot: Carrots are one of the richest food sources of beta-carotene (vitamin A)
Natural caramelisation during roasting develops a sweetness children love
Far healthier than regular chips with a fraction of the calories
recipes.kidTip: Present as 'carrot fries' not carrots — the rebranding works every time!
recipes.ageTip: Suitable for all ages. Soft when roasted — easy for toddlers.

recipes.name: Turkey & Squash Apple Hash

ingredients.item: Butternut squash or pumpkin, small diced

steps: Heat coconut oil in large skillet over medium-high heat.
Cook squash and onion 7–8 minutes until squash is tender.
Add garlic and cook 1 minute more.
Add turkey mince and cook until browned, breaking up as it cooks.
Add apple, cinnamon and sage. Stir to combine.
Cook 3 more minutes until apple softens.
Season and serve.
recipes.nutrition: Lean protein from turkey · Beta-carotene from squash · Apple adds natural sweetness
nutritionSnapshot: Turkey is one of the leanest protein sources available
Apple and cinnamon create natural sweetness that children love
Butternut squash provides beta-carotene and vitamin A
recipes.kidTip: The sweet apple and cinnamon make this smell incredible — kids will ask what's cooking.
recipes.ageTip: Soft textures throughout — suitable for all ages. Perfect autumn/winter meal.
